reasoning: Monaco is one of the safest and most intensely policed sovereign states in the world. ACLED only tracks political violence, riots, protests, and armed group violence against civilians. The base rate for such events producing a fatality in Monaco is virtually zero (has not happened in modern decades; previous high-profile deaths were individual crimes, not ACLED-relevant armed or political violence). Since no fatalities have occurred in the baseline window (360 days before March 2025), any single ACLED fatality in the 30-day window leading up to the resolution dates would trigger a 'YES'. However, the chances of an unexpected terror attack or violent mob fatality happening precisely in any given 30-day window remains vanishingly small. I am setting the probabilities at the minimum allowed (0.02), with slight increases for later dates to reflect marginal time-horizon uncertainty according to the platform instructions.
